mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or signal loses predictive power with p > 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term IC is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side bottlenecks and reduced corporate output, triggering negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not precede negative 5-day returns in late-cycle periods, or signal accuracy falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: High inflation readings fail to predict negative returns, or forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistical correlation with 20-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.01 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Weak quarterly GDP growth combined with declining personal consumption expenditures signals a demand contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined weak GDP and consumption signal yields positive returns in >55% of cases, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production breaking above its 90-day trend indicates accelerating manufacturing activity and improving capacity utilization, forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Breakouts above the 90-day average fail to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of instances, or signal decays within 10 days.
